## Sample DataFrame

The following CSV file, **class.csv**, is used to demonstrate pyLikeSAS functionality in all examples.

```c
Name,Sex,Age,Height,Weight
Alfred,M,14,69,112.5
Alice,F,13,56.5,84
Barbara,F,13,65.3,98
Carol,F,14,62.8,102.5
Henry,M,14,63.5,102.5
James,M,12,57.3,83
Jane,F,12,59.8,84.5
Janet,F,15,62.5,112.5
Jeffrey,M,13,62.5,84
John,M,12,59,99.5
Joyce,F,11,51.3,50.5
Judy,F,14,64.3,90
Louise,F,12,56.3,77
Mary,F,15,66.5,112
Philip,M,16,72,150
Robert,M,12,64.8,128
Ronald,M,15,67,133
Thomas,M,11,57.5,85
William,M,15,66.5,112
```

Its content is identical to the well‑known `SASHELP.CLASS` sample table.  
See the module `pyLikeSAS.sas_sample_datasets.py` if you need to download it.

After downloading, the CSV file can be read into a `pd.DataFrame` using the following code:

```python
# import core of pyLikeSAS 
import pandas as pd
from pyLikeSAS.PdDataSet import PdDataSet
from pyLikeSAS.lookup_format import lookup_format
from pyLikeSAS.proc_print import import proc_print
from pyLikeSAS.data_step import data_step

# read class.csv into pandas DataFrame
df = pd.read_csv(
    "./data/class.csv",
    dtype={'Weight':  float, 'Height':  float, }
)
```
